description:
Goal: replace separate type/planning phases with one root-scoped semantic fact fixpoint.

Signal:
- `SemanticClosed(root)` appears only when root semantic jobs are quiescent, required waiters are resolved, and semantic fact revisions are stable.
- Function definitions can exist without lowered bodies; lowered bodies can exist without root activation; entry dispatch and helper-guard facts can exist without body semantics; activation/executable facts appear only through reachable roots.
- Type facts, selected callees, return needs, executable specs, callable entries, and effects are co-derived by work-graph rules, not by multiple hidden phase loops.
- Ordered dispatch is planned once as compiler-owned data and reused by semantics, runtime matching, and later artifact work; Compiler2 does not grow a second matcher beside `dispatch_matrix`.

Spine:
1. Keep the drive/work graph small and move job families into focused modules.
2. Add a per-function `LoweredBody(function)` boundary owned by Compiler2.
3. Neutralize the shared `dispatch_matrix` boundary so Compiler2 can consume it directly without copying or old-world handle leakage.
4. Reify dispatch-pure helper functions into compiler-owned guard facts; this replaces `defguard`-style special cases.
5. Derive function-entry dispatch plans from clause heads, preconditions, and guards.
6. Derive direct-call semantic facts from dispatch-selected lowered bodies.
7. Add recursive convergence and retraction semantics.
8. Pull runtime/protocol/callable cases through the same facts.
9. Let artifacts consume `SemanticClosed` mechanically.
title: Compiler2 semantic closure: co-derive type and executable facts
